source.multipart_basis: Decomposition reflects CAWST construction sequence: base slab poured first, wire-mesh reinforcement cage erected and tied, then mortar applied in inner and outer plaster passes, with roof dome cast last and openings locally reinforced by embedded port fittings and an access hatch frame.

full_prompt:
Design one representative 5 m3 cylindrical ferrocement household water storage tank compliant with the CAWST Household Water Storage Tanks construction manual (2019). The tank is 2.0 m inner diameter, 1.6 m straight-wall height, with a flat (or slightly domed) ferrocement top and a ferrocement base slab, designed to store potable water at atmospheric pressure with a maximum fill head of 1.6 m from the base. Wall construction is ferrocement: 30 mm nominal wall thickness comprising three layers of 13 mm hexagonal chicken-wire mesh + two layers of 6 mm reinforcing bars at 150 mm each way (vertical + circumferential), embedded in a 1:3 cement:sand mortar of minimum compressive strength 25 MPa. The wall thickness schedule follows the CAWST tank manual: at any given depth from the free surface the hoop tensile stress induced in the cementitious composite by hydrostatic pressure must be checked against the tensile capacity of the ferrocement cross-section with a safety factor of at least 2 on hoop stress. For a 5 m3 tank (~2.0 m diameter, 1.6 m height, hydrostatic head up to 1.6 m giving gamma_w * h = 15.7 kPa at base) the maximum hoop force per unit height = p * r = 15.7 kPa * 1.0 m = 15.7 kN/m. The base slab is 80 mm ferrocement with equivalent reinforcement, supported on a prepared gravel pad. Inlet, outlet, overflow, and cleanout openings are locally reinforced. Target materials: mortar fc >= 25 MPa, steel mesh fy >= 250 MPa, rebar fy >= 420 MPa. Deliverable is a CAD assembly of the tank shell, base slab, top, and reinforcement representation, suitable for linear static structural FEA under hydrostatic self-weight + water load. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses are NOT required; hydrostatic pressure is a static gravity load.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This design is delivered as a multi-part assembly rather than a single solid body. The decomposition mirrors the CAWST ferrocement construction sequence: (A) base slab, (B) wire-mesh + rebar reinforcement cage, (C) outer mortar shell, (D) inner mortar shell, (E) roof dome, (F) inlet/outlet/overflow port fittings, (G) access hatch frame. The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design a 5 m3 cylindrical ferrocement household water tank as a multi-part assembly (base slab, wire-mesh + rebar reinforcement cage, outer mortar shell, inner mortar shell, roof dome, inlet/outlet/overflow port fittings, access hatch frame) per the CAWST Household Water Storage Tanks construction manual (2019).

Inner diameter 2.0 m, straight-wall height 1.6 m, nominal wall thickness 30 mm, base slab 80 mm ferrocement. Mortar fc >= 25 MPa; steel mesh fy >= 250 MPa; rebar 6 mm fy >= 420 MPa.

Load cases: LC1 hydrostatic full (head 1.6 m, pressure at base 15.7 kPa); LC2 self-weight. Pass/fail: SF >= 2 on hoop tensile stress at any depth; max hoop force per unit height 15.7 kN/m. FEM at assembled level; connection DCR <= 1.0.

prompt.title: 5 m3 ferrocement household water tank per CAWST (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a 2.0 m diameter x 1.6 m cylindrical ferrocement tank with 30 mm wall and SF 2 on hoop stress per CAWST manual. [Multi-part assembly] base_slab (x1), reinforcement_cage (x1), outer_mortar_shell (x1), inner_mortar_shell (x1), roof_dome (x1), port_fittings (x3+), access_hatch_frame (x1). FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under governing load case.

boundary_conditions.base_slab_bottom: supported on gravel pad, vertical reaction only

objective.primary: pass SF 2 on hoop stress + capacity check

parts.name: base_slab
parts.role: ferrocement foundation disk transferring tank + water weight to gravel pad
parts.material_hint: 1:3 cement:sand mortar fc >= 25 MPa with 6 mm rebar grid @ 150 mm e.w.

parts.name: reinforcement_cage
parts.role: cylindrical wire-mesh + rebar skeleton providing tensile hoop capacity
parts.material_hint: 3 layers 13 mm hex chicken-wire mesh (fy >= 250 MPa) + 6 mm rebar @ 150 mm e.w. (fy >= 420 MPa)

parts.name: outer_mortar_shell
parts.role: outer plaster pass encapsulating reinforcement cage and resisting hoop tension

parts.name: inner_mortar_shell
parts.role: inner plaster pass providing watertight surface and composite action with cage
parts.material_hint: 1:3 cement:sand mortar fc >= 25 MPa, smooth-trowel finish

parts.name: roof_dome
parts.role: ferrocement top closure, flat or slightly domed, casts over perimeter ring
parts.material_hint: ferrocement: mortar fc >= 25 MPa with mesh + 6 mm rebar radial bars

parts.name: port_fittings
parts.role: locally reinforced penetrations for inlet, outlet, overflow, cleanout
parts.material_hint: galvanized steel or PVC pipe stub cast through ferrocement wall with mesh wrap

parts.name: access_hatch_frame
parts.role: reinforced opening in roof for inspection and cleaning
parts.material_hint: 6 mm rebar frame embedded in ferrocement collar around opening

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: CAWST wall-thickness-vs-head schedule for H <= 2.0 m

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces <= 1.0 under LC1+LC2 governing load case.
Composite ferrocement wall (inner shell + cage + outer shell) must behave as a monolithic section in FEA; bond-slip between cage and mortar shells <= 0.1 mm at LC1.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: inner mortar shell D transfers vertical reaction + base hoop ring into base slab A through cold-joint cementitious bond
reinforcement cage B develops hoop tension into outer mortar shell C via mortar-to-steel bond
reinforcement cage B develops hoop tension into inner mortar shell D via mortar-to-steel bond
outer mortar shell C supports roof dome E at perimeter ring via cast-in-place cementitious joint
inner mortar shell D embeds port fittings F with mesh-wrapped collar; local hoop stress redistributed around opening
roof dome E receives access hatch frame G as cast-in-place embedded rebar lip

fem_check_policy.rationale: Ferrocement composite action between cage, mortar shells, and base slab can only be validated at the assembled level; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: CAWST construction sequence inherently produces discrete cast-and-cure stages (base slab, cage, outer plaster, inner plaster, roof dome) with embedded port fittings and hatch frame; each stage is a modeling-distinct body with a cold-joint interface.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] counts and interfaces[] reflect CAWST construction practice but are speculative for FEA decomposition, not explicitly prescribed by the manual.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
